text: Code reviews are a key part of modern software development. In a code review, a developer checks a colleague's code before it is added to the main project. This process helps catch bugs early, when they are easier and cheaper to fix. It also encourages knowledge sharing; team members learn about different parts of the codebase and pick up useful techniques from each other. Finally, regular code reviews improve overall team quality by building a shared standard for clean, maintainable code. The whole team takes pride in their work, and the software becomes more reliable.
